title: Analysis of Feature Extraction from Protein Sequences
subtitle: How can we extract the most useful features from a protein sequence?
description: This bachelor dissertation explores the process of extracting meaningful features from raw protein sequences, building an end-to-end pipeline for protein feature extraction.

The research investigates which feature extraction methods yield the most informative and discriminative representations for downstream machine learning tasks, comparing classical biochemical descriptors with modern embedding approaches.
supervisors: dr. ir. Pieter Coussement (ML6) — External Promotor
abstract: This dissertation analyses feature extraction techniques applied to protein sequences, constructing a pipeline that benchmarks multiple approaches. The central research question asks which features are most useful for downstream predictive modelling, examining classical physicochemical descriptors alongside modern sequence-embedding strategies.

title: Generative Scene Generation
subtitle: How does the geometric consistency of AI-generated videos affect the quality of 3D scene reconstruction using Gaussian Splatting?
description: This paper, produced for the course 'Individual Project' during the bridging program at UHasselt, investigates the relationship between the geometric coherence of AI-generated video and the fidelity of 3D scenes reconstructed via Gaussian Splatting.

The study builds a full pipeline from text/image-to-video generation through COLMAP-based structure-from-motion to 3DGS rendering, evaluating how geometric artifacts in synthetic footage propagate into the final 3D reconstruction.
program: Bridging Program — Master of Computer Science
abstract: This study examines how geometric consistency in AI-generated videos impacts the quality of 3D scene reconstruction via Gaussian Splatting. A pipeline is constructed from video generation through structure-from-motion to 3DGS rendering, revealing the degree to which synthetic geometric artifacts degrade or distort the resulting 3D scenes.
